etymology.description: Ťôę(Ťôę) (hu«ź, "insect") + ŤĽü(ŤĽü) (ru«én, "soft") = soft, without spine or hardness
etymology.interpretation: The character ū¶ėí (ru«én, "soft") combines the semantic component Ťôę(Ťôę) (hu«ź, "insect") suggesting something small or malleable, with the phonetic component ŤĽü(ŤĽü) (ru«én, "soft") which contributes the sound. Together, they suggest a quality of being easily compressed or lacking rigidity, much like an insect without strength or backbone. Over time, this representation evolved into the abstract concept of 'softness' in both physical and metaphorical senses.
